_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice priced relative to 2015 (set at 100), stands at 141, indicating a 41% price increase since 2015. This rise is substantial but uneven across components, reflecting a recent climb in certain commodities rather than a uniform inflationary trend. The index aggregates diverse soft commodities, whose prices are particularly sensitive to supply shocks.
notes.movers: Coffee prices nearly doubled, rising 99%, making it the primary driver of the basket’s increase. Orange juice followed with a 34% gain, while wheat and sugar saw more moderate increases of 19% and 13%, respectively. These movements are largely due to weather events and crop diseases such as frost and drought in arabica coffee regions and citrus greening affecting orange harvests, rather than broad economic or inflationary forces. Soft commodity markets are relatively thin, meaning smaller supply disruptions can cause significant price volatility.
notes.why: The overall headline rise in the basket is driven by a handful of weather-sensitive crops facing persistent supply challenges. Because these are soft commodities with thin markets, prices can remain elevated for extended periods when adverse conditions persist or reoccur. This underscores the role of specific environmental risks rather than general inflation dynamics in shaping the price trends observed.
